Notes: IMU. REPLACES ICM-42605 (C2655099) / ICM-42688-P (C1850418) — entire 426xx family is reel-only or OOS in small qty. Bosch BMI270 is in stock at LCSC at qty 1, ~$0.82-1.79. WARNING: DIFFERENT PINOUT from the 426xx — footprint changed to ...-BR and every IMU net was re-wired. Wiring (4-wire SPI, Secondary=None, datasheet Table 22 p.145): SCx(13)=SPI1 SCK/PA5, SDx(14)=MOSI/PA7, SDO(1)=MISO/PA6, CSB(12)=CS/PC4 + 10k pullup to VDDIO, INT1(4)=PC3/EXTI3. VDD(8)=+3V3_IMU +100nF, VDDIO(5)=+3V3 +100nF, GND(7)+GNDIO(6)=GND. ASDx(2)/ASCx(3)->VDDIO; OCSB(10)/OSDO(11)/INT2(9)=DNC. Decoupling is just 100nF x2 (NOT the 2.2uF/10nF combo the 426xx wanted). Betaflight gyro define = BMI270.

Blackbox flash 16MB, ~$0.36. BOYAMICRO BY25Q128ES is NAMED in Betaflight's supported flash list. SOIC-8-208mil = same footprint+pinout as W25Q128 (drop-in). Replaces Winbond C97521/C179171 which hit MOQ 12-14 (~$25-30). Backup: GD25Q128ESIG C2758105 (~$0.83, GigaDevice, also BF-supported). Avoid Puya (not in BF list).

Pin-for-pin drop-in for TPS5430DDAR (C9864, OOS). Same DDA/ESOP-8 + pinout + 1.221V ref (10k/3.24k still =5.0V), 5.5-36V in, 5A. All buck passives unchanged. If load >3A, upgrade catch diode D2 to 5A Schottky (SS54). Verified in stock.
Buzzer. Set DNP if not populating.

Sunlord SWPA6045S150MT 15uH, 6.0x6.0x4.5mm, ~2A Irms / 2.7A Isat, 71mOhm. MOQ 10 = ~$0.70, IN STOCK. Replaces 22uH C354622 (wrong value after 4S change + OOS + MOQ 10 @ $25). FOOTPRINT ACTION: not identical to the old land — new pads are at +/-2.60mm / 2.47x5.02mm vs old +/-2.50mm / 2.20x5.72mm. Import via libs/import_lcsc.py and repoint L2. For near-3A load use Bourns SRN6045TA-150M (C1330797).
3.3V zener clamp on ESC current-sense line into PC1 (1k series + zener). MDD BZT52C3V3 SOD-123. Verified.

Buck Cin, GRM32ER71H106KA12L 50V X7R. QTY CORRECTED 1->2 per locked design. TPS545x input RMS ripple = Iout/2 ~= 1.5A (datasheet 8.2.1.2.2); two caps halve per-part ripple current and lower effective ESR. 1210 is mandatory: 50V rating + DC-bias derating at 16.8V (a small pkg would derate below the 4.7uF datasheet minimum). ACTION: C6 must still be added to the schematic/board — only C5 is currently placed.

Buck Cout. Panasonic POSCAP 10TPB220M — the EXACT part named in the TPS543x/545x datasheet worked example (8.2.1.2.3.2). 220uF / 10V / ESR 40mOhm @100kHz / 3000mA ripple rating (design needs 143mA). REPLACES C156462 (KEMET T520): that part was 220uF but only 6.3V — too tight on a 5V rail since tantalum-family needs ~2x voltage derating (TI's reference is 10V) — and its 12mOhm ESR sat below the 20-40mOhm band the TPS545x internal compensation assumes, pushing the ESR zero from ~18kHz to ~60kHz past crossover. Case EIA-7343 (2917) 7.3x4.3x3.8mm: same land pattern as before, but 3.8mm tall (was ~2mm) so footprint bumped -20 -> -31; confirm nothing overhangs it.
MERGED: C7 was '.01uF'=10nF. One is buck BOOT cap. Samsung 50V X7R. Verified.
Two are STM32 VCAP. Fenghua 0805F225M500NT 50V. Verified.
MERGED: C11 was '.1uF'=100nF. YAGEO 100nF 50V X7R. Verified.
Crystal load caps. Samsung 22pF 50V C0G. CONFIRM value vs crystal CL. Verified.

UNI-ROYAL 10k 1% 0805. R7 (buck feedback upper) MERGED IN from the separate 0603 line — no electrical reason for a smaller package: it dissipates ~1.4uW (378uA through the 10k/3.24k divider) and sees 3.78V. What actually matters per TPS545x 10.1 is placement: keep R7/R8 close to the VSENSE pin and route the VOUT->VSENSE trace away from the PH node. Both R7 and R8 must be 1% (they set Vout). Verified.
